title: Mandevilla vanheurckii
taxonomy: Mandevilla vanheurckii is classified in the kingdom Plantae, in the order Gentianales, in the family Apocynaceae, and in the genus Mandevilla. This classification places Mandevilla vanheurckii in the same family as other plants like the common oleander and the queen of the night. The order Gentianales is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, including the apocynaceous, gentianaceous, and loganiaceous families. Mandevilla vanheurckii's classification reflects its unique characteristics and its relationships with other flowering plants. Further study and classification may be necessary to fully understand the place of Mandevilla vanheurckii in the plant kingdom.
uses: Mandevilla vanheurckii has been found to have potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine.
